message:
feat(widgets): grapheme-aware title fitting, scissor-respecting styles, soft-wrap page nav, and accessibility hardening

block.rs:
- Replace text_width() with fitted_text_width() that iterates graphemes
  and stops when the next grapheme would exceed available width, fixing
  right-aligned wide-character (CJK) titles that previously overflowed
  or were misaligned when the last grapheme straddled the boundary
- Early-return when fitted display_width is 0 (avoids rendering empty
  title spans that could corrupt border characters)
- Clear the title span with Cell::default() before draw_text_span() in
  the Widget impl so that NoStyling degradation truly drops border
  styling instead of inheriting it from pre-existing cells
- Move Accessible impl above tests (module layout consistency with
  scrollbar.rs and spinner.rs)
- Add test: render_title_right_aligned_truncated_wide_grapheme_uses_fitted_width
- Add test: degradation_no_styling_keeps_border_when_title_does_not_fit

input.rs:
- Convert imperative A11yState field assignments to struct-init syntax
  with ..Default for clarity and consistency with other widgets

lib.rs (set_style_area):
- Intersect the target area with the buffer's current scissor rect
  before iterating, so style application respects clipping regions
- Apply the buffer's current opacity to fg/bg colors before writing,
  enabling translucent overlay compositing to work correctly with
  set_style_area (previously only draw_text_span honored opacity)
- Use row_cells_mut_span() for bulk row mutation instead of per-cell
  get_mut(), reducing dirty-tracking overhead from O(cells) mark calls
  to O(rows)
- Convert StyleFlags from attrs once outside the loop instead of per-cell
- Add tests: set_style_area_respects_scissor, set_style_area_respects_opacity_stack

list.rs:
- Rustfmt normalization of A11yNodeInfo builder chain line breaks

paragraph.rs:
- Add truncate_accessible_text() helper that truncates on grapheme
  boundaries (not byte boundaries) to a 200-char limit with "..."
  suffix, preventing panics on multi-byte grapheme clusters (e.g.
  CJK, combining characters) that previously could slice mid-grapheme
- Apply truncation to both name and description fields (previously only
  name was truncated, and description was unbounded)
- Move Accessible impl above tests for layout consistency
- Use .lines().iter() instead of .lines().map() to match upstream API
- Add tests: accessibility_truncates_long_unicode_without_panicking,
  accessibility_truncates_description_when_block_title_present,
  accessibility_preserves_exactly_200_chars_without_ellipsis,
  accessibility_truncates_on_grapheme_boundaries

progress.rs:
- Convert imperative A11yState field assignments to struct-init syntax
- Fix MiniBar percent-only rendering in tight widths: trim leading
  space from percent text so "50%" renders as "50%" not " 50%" when
  bar_width is 0 and only percent text is shown (both normal and
  EssentialOnly degradation paths)
- Add tests: minibar_render_percent_only_starts_with_digits_in_tight_widths,
  minibar_essential_only_percent_starts_with_digits_in_tight_widths

scrollbar.rs:
- Move Accessible impl above tests (no logic change)

spinner.rs:
- Move Accessible impl above tests (no logic change)

textarea.rs:
- Rewrite page_up/page_down to be soft-wrap-aware: when soft_wrap is
  enabled and viewport width is known, use move_cursor_visual_up/down
  instead of raw editor.move_up/down, so page navigation moves by
  visual rows (wrapped lines) rather than logical lines
- Compute text_area_width by subtracting gutter_width from viewport
  width, ensuring line-number gutters don't cause off-by-N navigation
- Fall back to per-line movement when viewport width is 0 or soft_wrap
  is disabled

textarea_edge_cases.rs:
- Add tests: page_navigation_without_soft_wrap_uses_state_viewport_height,
  page_navigation_with_line_numbers_uses_text_area_width,
  soft_wrap_page_navigation_moves_by_visual_rows,
  soft_wrap_page_navigation_uses_text_width_without_render

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
